---
title: "Equivalence with tna: synthetic balanced"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>",
  message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10, fig.height = 7, dpi = 150
)
source("_equivalence-helpers.R")
```

20 simulated sessions (length 8--14) with a balanced 3+3 alphabet
(`H1, H2, H3` for Human, `A1, A2, A3` for AI). Two transition rows are
biased (H1 strongly prefers A1; A2 prefers H1 and A3); the rest is
uniform. This is a small but non-trivial sanity-check dataset.

The pipeline below runs the same htna ↔ tna ↔ Nestimate three-way
equivalence checks as the
[main equivalence article](tna-equivalence.html).

## Setup

```{r}
library(tna)
library(htna)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

ds <- ds_synthetic_balanced()
```

```{r child = "_equivalence-body.Rmd"}
```
